heading: Definitions
label: 3601 Definitions
notes: References in TextAn Act to provide for the conservation and management of the fisheries, and for other purposes, approved ( et seq.), referred to in par. (1), is , , , known as the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act, which is classified principally to chapter 38 (§ 1801 et seq.) of this title. children.text: “Act of 1976” means the Act entitled “An Act to provide for the conservation and management of the fisheries, and for other purposes”, approved ( et seq.);April 13, 197616 U.S.C. 1801
“Commission” means any of the Commissions of the Organization that are established by the Convention;
“Commissioner” means a United States Commissioner appointed under ;section 3602 of this title
“Convention” means the Convention for the Conservation of Salmon in the North Atlantic Ocean, signed at Reykjavik, Iceland, on ;March 2, 1982
“Council” means the Council established by the Convention;
“fishing” has the same meaning as such term has in section 3(10) of the Act of 1976 ();116 U.S.C. 1802(10)See References in Text note below.1
“Organization” means the North Atlantic Salmon Conservation Organization established under the Convention;
“person” has the same meaning as such term has in section 3(19) of the Act of 1976 (); and116 U.S.C. 1802(19)
“salmon” means all species of salmon which migrate in or into the waters of the Atlantic Ocean north of 36 degrees north latitude.
